name: markerObject
signature: int markerObject(QString objectName, bool visible, QString mtype, QString color, float size, bool autoDelete, int autoDeleteTimeoutMs)
description:
Creates a visual marker attached to a celestial object (star, planet, galaxy, etc.). The marker follows the object as it moves across the sky, making it useful for highlighting specific objects in presentations or educational content.

**Note:** The marker is placed on the object's J2000 coordinates at creation time and will undergo aberration correction.

parameters.name: objectName
parameters.description: English name of the celestial object (e.g., 'Mars', 'Sirius', 'Andromeda Galaxy').

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: `true` to start visible, `false` to start hidden.

parameters.name: mtype
parameters.description: Marker shape. Options: `'cross'`, `'circle'`, `'ellipse'`, `'square'`, `'dotted-circle'`, `'circled-cross'` (or `'circled-plus'`), `'dashed-square'`, `'squared-dotted-circle'` (or `'squared-dcircle'`), `'crossed-circle'`, `'target'`, `'gear'`, `'disk'`.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: HTML-like color (e.g., `'#ffff00'` for yellow, `'#ff6666'` for light red).

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Marker size in pixels (recommended: 4-20).

parameters.name: autoDelete
parameters.description: If `true`, the marker is deleted after being displayed once.

parameters.name: autoDeleteTimeoutMs
parameters.description: If non-zero, the marker is automatically deleted after this many milliseconds.

returnDescription: Unique marker ID, or `-1` if the object was not found.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: markerEquatorial
signature: int markerEquatorial(QString RA, QString Dec, bool j2000epoch, bool visible, QString mtype, QString color, float size, bool autoDelete, int autoDeleteTimeoutMs, bool withAberration)
description:
Creates a visual marker fixed to a specific Equatorial coordinate (Right Ascension/Declination). The marker stays at that celestial position, useful for marking deep-sky object positions, exoplanet host stars, or other points of interest.

**Note:** The marker is placed at the converted J2000 coordinates at creation time. It is not possible to create a marker in the coordinates of the current date that changes with precession.

parameters.name: j2000epoch
parameters.description: If `true`, coordinates are treated as J2000.0 epoch. If `false`, they represent the equinox of the current date and will be converted to J2000 at creation time.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: `true` to start visible, `false` to start hidden.

parameters.name: mtype
parameters.description: Marker shape (see `markerObject` for options).

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: HTML-like color.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Marker size in pixels.

parameters.name: autoDelete
parameters.description: If `true`, the marker is deleted after being displayed once.

parameters.name: autoDeleteTimeoutMs
parameters.description: If non-zero, the marker is automatically deleted after this many milliseconds.

parameters.name: withAberration
parameters.description: If `true`, coordinates are subject to aberration correction. If `false`, they represent strict frame coordinates.

returnDescription: Unique marker ID, or `-1` if creation failed.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: markerHorizon
signature: int markerHorizon(QString az, QString alt, bool visible, QString mtype, QString color, float size, bool autoDelete, int autoDeleteTimeoutMs)
description: Creates a visual marker fixed to a specific Altitude/Azimuth (horizontal) coordinate. The marker stays at that compass direction and altitude as the sky rotates, useful for marking landscape features, cardinal directions, or telescope pointing targets.

parameters.name: alt
parameters.description: Altitude angle. `'0°'` = horizon, `'90°'` = zenith. (e.g., `'25d30m30s'`).

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: `true` to start visible, `false` to start hidden.

parameters.name: mtype
parameters.description: Marker shape (see `markerObject` for options).

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: HTML-like color.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Marker size in pixels.

parameters.name: autoDelete
parameters.description: If `true`, the marker is deleted after being displayed once.

parameters.name: autoDeleteTimeoutMs
parameters.description: If non-zero, the marker is automatically deleted after this many milliseconds.

returnDescription: Unique marker ID, or `-1` if creation failed.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getMarkerShow
description: Returns whether a marker is currently visible.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique marker ID.

returnDescription: `true` if the marker is visible, `false` if hidden or the marker does not exist.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setMarkerShow
description: Shows or hides an existing marker. The marker will fade in/out smoothly.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique marker ID.

parameters.name: show
parameters.description: `true` to show the marker, `false` to hide it.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: deleteMarker
description: Permanently deletes a marker. Does nothing if the marker ID does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique marker ID to delete.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: deleteAllMarkers
description: Deletes all custom markers created by the script. Useful for cleaning up at the end of a presentation or script.
returnDescription: The number of markers that were deleted.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.
